XRay: A visual effect making the model partially transparent to see underlying parts.
Guitar Mode: A mode related to guitar playing animation or control.
Spectator: An NPC or non-interactive character (not the user/viewer)
Actor Playlist: A list or sequence of actors to be loaded or played in DanceXR
Pro: A paid license tier of DanceXR (not the general word professional)
Retainable: Morphs or textures that persist after a reset
Ignore rotation of attach bone: Do not apply the rotation of the attach bone to the attached object
Occlusion: In AR mode, the visual blocking of virtual objects by real-world objects
Swing By Time: A method to modulate output over a time interval with a swinging motion
Feature Effect: Typo in the app for Feather Effect
Bonciness: Typo in the app for Bounciness: how much an object bounces
Stablizer: Typo in the app for Stabilizer: reduces camera jitter or shake
Visulize: Typo in the app for Visualize: shows a debug view of the colliders
Sex Overlay: A procedural motion layered on top of the current motion and pose
Concert Mode: A camera mode that stays at a fixed distance from the stage
Freefly Cam: A camera mode allowing free movement in all directions
Beat Cycle: A rhythmic interval used for timing camera movements to music
Tracking Mode: Camera movement that continuously follows a target
Center / Stage Center: The center point of the stage or performance environment
Prime Motion: The main motion assigned to an actor
Passthrough: AR mode displaying the real-world environment through device cameras
Glow w/ Music: Glow effect synchronized with music rhythm or beats
VR 120 3D: Virtual Reality video with 120-degree field of view in stereoscopic 3D
Slither: A motion style describing smooth, snake-like movement
room: A virtual online chatroom within DanceXR (not a physical room)
Shadow Only: An invisible object that only casts a shadow in the scene
Primitive Object: A basic geometric 3D shape (cube, sphere, cylinder, etc.)
stage ring: A ring of lights physically arranged around the stage
Prepass: An initial rendering pass done before the main render to prepare data
Glow Box: A DanceXR lighting effect simulating glowing box lights
Gloss Circle: A DanceXR circular highlight effect representing glossiness
Indoor (Sky Mode): A sky mode intended for indoor environment lighting simulation
Stage / Pool: A performance stage or the pool area adjacent to it
V Shape: A V-shaped edge type used in certain animation or visual effects
Trackpad Mode: An input mode that simulates joystick input using a trackpad device
Allocation of movement between male and: How much of the total motion is assigned to the male vs. female character
[Zero]: Resets the character model to its standard neutral pose
Unknown Bones: Bones in the model not recognized or mapped by the system
Lock 0 Limit: Lock the joint when its movement limit is set to zero
Target Actor: The character model used as reference for a system or effect
Dressing System: DanceXR system for managing character clothing and outfit changes
Dissolve: A visual effect where an outfit fades or disappears gradually
Static Inclusive / Static Exclusive / Dy: Collision group settings: whether static or dynamic objects are included or excluded from collisions with each other
Sweat Collision: Physics interaction between sweat droplets and character surfaces
Compliance: In cloth simulation, how much particles respect their constraints (lower = stiffer)
Firmness A: Joint stiffness coefficient on one side of the movement range
Firmness B: Joint stiffness coefficient on the opposite side of the movement range
Reducing mass at each level: Progressively decreasing mass through a bone hierarchy to improve simulation performance
Reduction Ratio: The mass reduction factor applied per hierarchy level
Softbody: A deformable 3D object simulated as a soft material (e.g., cloth, jelly)
Allocation: In spotlight settings, which character the spotlight is assigned to
Adaptation: Automatic exposure adjustment in response to lighting changes
LED Mode: A display mode where a surface mimics an LED screen appearance
Wrap Mode: How a texture tiles when UV coordinates exceed the 0-1 range (repeat, clamp, mirror)
Fit Texture: Automatically scale the texture to fit the geometry
Audio Visualizer: DanceXR built-in tool to visualize audio waveforms or frequencies
Recursive: Ray tracing mode where rays are cast multiple times for multi-bounce reflections
Max Depth: Maximum number of bounce levels in ray tracing
Ray Length: Maximum travel distance of a ray before termination in ray tracing
Probe: A sampled point used for environment reflections or lighting calculations
Actor Floor: The floor area designated for character models to stand on
Paper White: The brightness level of white in HDR display configuration
Realtime Planar: Real-time planar reflections rendered each frame
Custom Inherit Motion: A bone setting defining how motion is inherited from a parent bone with custom rules
Relative To Floor: Position or rotation measured using the floor as the reference plane
Max Twist: Maximum allowed rotational angle on the leg or foot twist axis
Pairing: Linking two characters together for synchronized interaction or partner motion
Acceleration Mode: A physics mode controlling how forces or velocities are applied to objects
Propagate: Apply a setting change to all child bones or joints in the hierarchy
InfluenceBone: A bone that drives or influences the movement of other bones during animation
Sole Height: The height of the foot sole relative to the ground plane
Special Shader Mode: A unique DanceXR rendering mode applying specific visual effects to a model
GlowColorData: Internal data structure holding color parameters for a glow effect
GroundTex: The texture applied to the ground plane
Use Accessory Position: Whether to factor in an attached accessory position when calculating pose
Entitlement: License or permission granting access to specific DanceXR features or content
Gradual Enable: Slowly activates cloth simulation over time to prevent sudden jumps
String Connection: Horizontal connections between vertices in cloth string simulation mode
Bend Penis: Animation parameter controlling penile bending for physics-based motion
Sex Motion: Animation sequences related to sexual interactions between characters
Cowgirl Sex: A sexual position with the female character on top facing the partner
Sex From Behind: A sexual position performed from behind the character
Jito: A specific facial expression blendshape; meaning depends on model and may require clarification
Dummy Read Speed: A placeholder read speed value used for simulation or testing purposes
Output Buffer: Reserved memory area where recorded data is stored before saving or processing
Projector Scene Example: A built-in DanceXR example scene using a projector to display video
LED Screen Example: A built-in DanceXR example scene using an LED screen to display video
Reduction Rate: A multiplier that reduces applied forces or speeds in physics simulation
Background Vibration: A subtle movement effect applied to background elements to simulate vibration
Foreground Vibration: A subtle movement effect applied to foreground elements to simulate vibration
Motion Curve: A curve that defines the speed or progression of an animation over time
Feature Page: A documentation page highlighting a specific DanceXR feature
Sit Back & Enjoy: DanceXR auto-play mode where the app selects and plays content automatically
